emails.subject: The Only Friendsgiving Menu You Need
emails.content:
Bon Appetit

The Only Friendsgiving Menu You Need

You're hosting. We've got you.

Citrus-Herb Butter Turkey
The secret is getting the butter under the skin the night before. Lemon zest, thyme, rosemary, garlic. Roast at 325 for about 3 hours for a 12-lb bird.

Butternut Squash Soup (Vegan!)
Sweet, creamy, and totally plant-based. Roast the squash first for deeper flavor. Coconut milk instead of cream.

Classic Green Bean Casserole
Forget the canned soup version. Make the mushroom cream sauce from scratch. Top with fried shallots.

Brown Butter Mashed Potatoes
Brown your butter first. Game changer. Yukon Golds only.

Cranberry Sauce with Orange
Whole berries, sugar, fresh orange juice and zest. 15 minutes on the stove. Done.

See all recipes at bonappetit.com/friendsgiving

Bon Appetit

emails.subject: [isaramos/fintrack-py] CSV import fails with Unicode characters (#23)
emails.content:
devjorge opened this issue:

When importing a CSV with accented characters (e.g. "Cafe", "Jose Tacos") the parser throws a UnicodeDecodeError.

Steps to reproduce:
1. Create CSV with non-ASCII characters in description field
2. Run fintrack import transactions.csv
3. UnicodeDecodeError: ascii codec can not decode byte 0xc3

Looks like the CSV reader needs encoding=utf-8. Happy to submit a fix.

Python 3.11, macOS Sonoma.

---

emails.subject: [isaramos/fintrack-py] Fix CSV parser memory leak for large files (#34)
emails.content:
chen-dev opened this pull request:

Current CSV parser loads the entire file into memory. For large transaction histories (10K+ rows) this causes significant memory usage and occasional crashes.

Switched to a streaming parser that processes in chunks of 500. Memory usage drops from ~800MB to ~15MB for my test file (47K transactions).

Benchmarks:
- 1K rows: 0.3s (no change)
- 10K rows: 2.1s to 1.8s
- 47K rows: OOM to 4.2s

All existing tests pass.

---

emails.content:

emails.subject: Re: Mortgage pre-approval question
emails.content:
Hi Isabella,

Happy to help you guys get started.

Based on what you've shared, you're in solid shape for the $450-550K range. Here's what I'm seeing right now:

Current rates (as of 2/12):
- 30-year fixed: 6.375% (5% down)
- 30-year fixed: 6.125% (10% down)
- FHA 30-year: 5.875% (3.5% down, but PMI is higher)

For a $500K purchase at 5% down ($25K):
- Monthly P&I: ~$2,967
- PMI: ~$185/mo (drops off at 20% equity)
- Property tax + insurance: figure $500-600/mo
- Total PITI: ~$3,650-3,750/mo

For pre-approval I'd need:
- Last 2 pay stubs (both of you)
- 2024 and 2025 W-2s
- Last 2 months bank statements (all accounts)
- Auto loan statement
- Driver's licenses

Also worth looking into: MassHousing has a first-time buyer program with lower PMI rates. I can run those numbers too.

Pre-approval takes 3-5 business days once I have everything. Let me know.

Best,
Tom Morrison
Senior Mortgage Loan Officer
Rockland Trust
(617) 555-0694
NMLS #284571
